slug: 太宰治为什么自杀了五次_人间失格_背后的人
title: 太宰治为什么自杀了五次——《人间失格》背后的人

outline.concept: Self-alienation as survival: some people learn to play a 'clown' role to avoid rejection, but the mask becomes a prison — they lose access to their own real feelings because they've never practiced having them honestly.
outline.query: 讨好别人 假装开心 伪装 社交面具 害怕被讨厌

outline.concept: Shame spirals: when a person's core belief is 'I am defective,' every failure confirms it and even success feels fraudulent. The shame doesn't respond to evidence — it consumes evidence.
outline.query: 觉得自己很烂 不配 搞砸了 自我厌恶 冒名顶替

outline.concept: Addiction as self-medication for emotional numbness: alcohol and drugs don't create escape — they fill an emptiness that the person cannot feel any other way. The substance becomes the only reliable relationship.
outline.query: 喝酒 酗酒 喝醉 逃避 麻痹自己 停不下来

outline.concept: Repeated suicide attempts as communication: each attempt screams 'I am in pain' in the only language that feels heard, but each one also normalizes the act — making the final, completed suicide easier to carry out.
outline.query: 自杀念头 自残 想死 活不下去 伤害自己

outline.concept: The artist's paradox: writing about one's own destruction requires enough distance to craft it into art, but that same distance can make the writer feel their suffering is only valuable when aestheticized — that they are worth more as material than as a person.
outline.query: 写作 创作 痛苦 灵感 把痛苦写成作品 表达
